1: The Lord gave his message to the prophet Zechariah in the second year that Darius ruled Persia as king. It happened in the eighth month of that year. Zechariah was the son of Berekiah and the grandson of Iddo.
This was the Lord 's message: I, the Lord, was very angry with your ancestors.
So you must say to the people, ‘The Lord Almighty says, “You must turn back to me, and I will return to you.” That is the message from the Lord Almighty.
He says, “Do not be like your ancestors. Many years ago the prophets told them, ‘Stop doing those evil and wicked things.’ But they refused to obey me.
Your ancestors have been dead for a long time. Those prophets also are no longer alive.

But my commands and my laws which the prophets spoke to the people are still true. I punished your ancestors, as I had warned them. Then they turned back to me. They said, ‘The Lord Almighty has punished us as we deserved for our sins. He has done as he said he would do.’ ” That is what the Lord says.’

On the 24th day of Shebat, the 11th month of the year, the Lord gave his message to the prophet Zechariah, the son of Berekiah and the grandson of Iddo. This happened in the second year that Darius ruled as king.
During the night I had a vision. I saw a man who was sitting on a red horse. The horse was standing among some myrtle trees in a valley. Behind him there were red, brown and white horses.
I asked the angel who was talking with me, ‘Please sir, what is the meaning of these horses?’ The angel said to me, ‘I will show you what they are.’
Then the man who was standing among the myrtle trees said, ‘The Lord has sent these horses to go and look in all the places on the earth.’

The men on the other horses agreed with the angel of the who was standing among the myrtle trees. They said, ‘We have gone to look at things all over the earth. We have seen that everywhere is now resting in peace.’

Then the angel of the Lord said, ‘ Lord Almighty, how long will you be angry with Jerusalem before you are kind to them? How long will you be angry with the cities of Judah? You have already been angry with them for 70 years.’
The Lord answered the angel who was speaking to me. He spoke kind words to the angel.
The angel then spoke to me again. He said, ‘Give this message to the people. Tell them clearly that the Lord Almighty says, “I love Jerusalem and Mount Zion very much. It is my special place.
I am very angry with the nations which think that they are safe. I was a little bit angry with my own people, but those nations caused my people to suffer too much.”

So this is what the Lord says: “Now I will return to Jerusalem and I will be kind to the people there. I will build my house there again.” That is what the Lord Almighty says. “Builders will measure the land in the city and they will build houses there again.”

Tell the people clearly that the Lord Almighty says, “The towns where my people live will have great riches again. I will cause the people of Zion to live in peace. I will show that I have chosen Jerusalem as my own special city.” ’
Then I looked up and I saw four horns.

I asked the angel who was speaking with me, ‘What are these horns?’ The angel replied, ‘These are the powerful enemies that took away the people of Judah, the people of Israel and the people of Jerusalem.’
Then the Lord showed me four workers with hammers.

I said, ‘What are these men coming to do?’ The Lord answered, ‘The horns are the enemies that took away Judah's people so that they have become helpless. But the workers have come to frighten the nations that have attacked Judah. The workers will take away the power of those nations.’
2: Then I looked up and I saw a young man in front of me. He had a string in his hand that he could use to measure things.
I asked the man, ‘Where are you going?’ He replied, ‘I am going to measure Jerusalem. I want to see how wide it is and how long it is.’
The angel who had been speaking with me now went away from me. Another angel came to meet him.

The second angel said to the first angel, ‘Run to that young man. Tell him, “Jerusalem will no longer be a city that has walls around it. This is because there will be many people and many animals that live in it.
The Lord himself promises that he will protect Jerusalem. He will be like a wall of fire around the city. His own glory will shine inside the city.” ’
This is the Lord 's message: ‘Run away! Escape from the country in the north where you have been prisoners. I sent you away in every direction, north, south, east and west.
Now escape, you people of Zion! Return home from Babylon where you have been living.’

Return home, because the Lord Almighty has sent me, Zechariah, with a message. He has sent me to warn the nations who have robbed you. He will use me to bring glory to himself. You are his special people. If anyone hurts you, it is the same as if they hurt God's own eye.
The Lord says, ‘I will use my power to punish your enemies. Even their own slaves will rob them.’ When that happens, you will know that the Lord Almighty has sent me to you.
The Lord says, ‘Sing and be happy, you people of Zion. Yes, I am coming to you! I will live among you.

At that time, people will come from many nations to worship me, the Lord. They will become my people too. And I will make my home among you all.’ When that happens, you will know that the Lord Almighty has sent me to you.
Judah will again belong to the Lord as his special place in his own land. He will choose Jerusalem as his own city.
So be quiet now, people everywhere, as you stand in front of the Lord. He is ready to move from his holy home in heaven to do great things.
3: In another vision I saw Joshua, the leader of the priests. He was standing in front of the angel of the. Satan stood at the right side of Joshua. He was ready to say that Joshua had done bad things.

The Lord said to Satan, ‘The Lord says that you are wrong, Satan. The Lord has chosen Jerusalem for himself. He says that you are wrong! Joshua is like a stick that someone pulls quickly out of the fire to keep it safe.’
Joshua was wearing very dirty clothes when he stood there, in front of the angel.
The angel said to the people who stood around him, ‘Remove his dirty clothes.’ Then he said to Joshua, ‘See what I have done. I have taken away your sin. Now I will give you new clothes to wear.’
Then I said, ‘Put a clean hat on his head.’ So they put a clean hat on his head and they dressed him in new clothes. The angel of the Lord stood near to them while they did this.
Then the angel of the Lord spoke seriously to Joshua.

He said, ‘The Lord Almighty says to you, “You must live in ways that please me. You must do the things that I tell you to do. If you do that, you will rule over my temple and all its yards. I will allow you to come with the angels who are standing around you.

Joshua, leader of the priests, you and all the other priests who are sitting with you must listen carefully. You are all like a sign of what will happen in the future. Yes, I will bring my servant, who is called the Branch.

Look! I have put a big stone in front of Joshua! It has seven sides. I will cut the words of a message in the stone. The message is that I will remove the sin of this guilty land in one day.” That is what the Lord Almighty says.
He says, “On that day, you will all ask your friends to come and sit with you. They will sit safely with you under your own vines and fig trees.” ’
4: Then the angel who had been speaking with me returned. He woke me up, like someone who had been asleep.
He asked me, ‘What do you see?’ I answered, ‘I see a lampstand that is made of gold. It has a bowl on top and there are seven lamps. There are seven pipes that take oil from the bowl to each lamp.
I also see two olive trees that are standing beside the lampstand. One tree is on the right side of the bowl and one tree is on the left side.’
I asked the angel who was speaking with me, ‘What do these lamps mean, sir?’
He said, ‘Do you not know what these lamps are?’ I said, ‘No, sir, I do not know.’

Then the angel said to me, ‘These lamps are a picture of the message that the Lord says to Zerubbabel: “Your own strength and your own power will not give you success. It is my Spirit that will give you success.” That is what the Lord Almighty says.

It may seem that a great mountain stands in front of Zerubbabel. But it will become flat ground! One day, Zerubbabel will bring the last stone to finish the temple building. Then the people will shout, “God is kind! He will bless it!” ’
Then the Lord 's message came to me again. He said,
‘Zerubbabel has built the foundation of the temple. He will also finish the building.’ When that happens, you will know that the Lord Almighty has sent me to you.

It may seem that only a little work has begun. But do not think that it is useless. The seven eyes of the Lord see everything that happens in the world. They will see Zerubbabel as he continues the work on the temple building. That will cause everyone to be happy.
Then I asked the angel, ‘What are these two olive trees that stand on the right side and on the left side of the lampstand?’
I also asked him, ‘I see two branches of the olive trees that are pouring olive oil through the two gold pipes. What is the meaning of those branches?’
The angel replied, ‘Do you not know what they are?’ I said, ‘No, sir, I do not know.’
So the angel said, ‘They are the two leaders that the Lord of the whole earth has chosen to serve him.’
5: I looked up again. I saw a scroll that was flying in the air.
The angel asked me, ‘What do you see?’ I answered, ‘I see a scroll that is flying in the air. It is about 10 metres long and 5 metres wide.’

The angel said to me, ‘The words on the scroll are a curse that is going out over the whole land. The curse tells about punishment for people who rob other people. God will remove them from the land. God will also remove from the land those people who tell lies against other people.

The Lord Almighty says, “I will send out this scroll to take a curse into the house of robbers. I will also send it into the house of people who use my name to tell lies. The curse will remain in their houses. It will completely destroy all the wood and the stones of their houses.” ’
Then the angel who was speaking to me came forward. He said, ‘Look up. See the thing that has now appeared.’
I asked, ‘What is it?’ He said, ‘It is a basket that people use to measure grain.’ He also said, ‘It shows the sin of all the people in the land.’
There was a heavy metal lid that covered the basket. As I looked, the angel lifted up the lid. I could see a woman who was sitting in the basket.
The angel said, ‘This woman shows what sin is.’ Then he pushed her down into the basket again. He put the heavy metal lid back on top of the basket.
Then I looked up and I saw two women. They had strong wings, like the wings of a large bird. The wind carried them towards me. They lifted the big basket up high into the sky.
I asked the angel who was speaking with me, ‘Where will these women take the basket?’

The angel said to me, ‘They will take it to the country of Babylon. They will build a temple for it there. When the temple is ready, they will put the basket in it. They will put it in a place of honour there.’
6: I looked up again and I saw four chariots. They came out from between two mountains that were made of bronze.
The first chariot had red horses to pull it. The second chariot had black horses.
The third chariot had white horses. The fourth chariot had grey horses. All the horses were strong.
Then I asked the angel who was speaking with me, ‘What are these chariots, sir?’
The angel answered me, ‘These are the four spirits of heaven. They have come from the Lord of all the earth.
The chariot with the black horses is going towards the north country. The chariot with the white horses is going after them. The chariot with the grey horses is going towards the south country.’

When these strong horses came out, they were in a hurry to leave. They wanted to travel everywhere on the earth. The Lord had told them, ‘Go and look everywhere in the whole earth.’ So that is what the horses did.
Then the Lord called to me. He said, ‘Look! The horses that have gone to the north country have caused my Spirit to have peace. I am no longer angry about the north country.’
The Lord gave this message to me:

‘Three men have come back from Babylon, where they were prisoners. Their names are Heldai, Tobijah and Jedaiah. Take the gifts that they have brought with them. Then go immediately to the house of Zephaniah's son, Josiah.
Use some of the gold and silver to make a royal crown. Then put it on the head of Jehozadak's son, Joshua, the leader of the priests.
Say to Joshua, “The Lord Almighty says, ‘Here is the man whose name is the Branch. He will grow and become great in this place. He will build the Lord 's temple.

Yes, he is the one who will build the Lord 's temple. He will wear royal clothes. He will sit on his throne and he will rule his people as their king. There will be a priest with him on his throne. They will both completely agree with each other as they work together.’ ”
They will put the crown in the Lord 's temple. It will cause people to give honour to Heldai, Tobijah, Jedaiah and Zephaniah's son Josiah.

People will come to Jerusalem from places that are far away. They will help to build the Lord 's temple. When this happens, you will know that the Lord Almighty has sent me to you. It will happen if you remember to obey the commands of the Lord your God.’
7: In the fourth year that Darius was king, the Lord spoke to Zechariah. It happened on the fourth day of the ninth month that is called Kislev.
At that time, the people of Bethel had sent Sharezer and Regem-Melech and their men to Jerusalem. They came to ask the Lord to bless them.

They came to the temple of the Lord Almighty to speak to the priests and to the prophets. They asked, ‘Should we continue to weep and to fast in the fifth month each year? We have done this for many years.’
The Lord Almighty then gave this message to me.

He said, ‘Speak to all the people in this land and to the priests. Tell them, “You were prisoners in another country for 70 years. At that time, you were sad and you fasted in the fifth month and in the seventh month of each year. But you did not really do this to give me honour.
Also, when you eat and you drink at special times, you do that to please yourselves.” ’

This is the same message from the Lord that the prophets spoke in past years. At that time, many people lived in Jerusalem and they were happy. Many people also lived in the towns around Jerusalem, in the Negev region and in the low hills in the west.
The Lord spoke again to Zechariah.
He said, ‘This is what the Lord Almighty says to the people: “Be sure that you do what is right and fair. Be kind and help each other.
Do not be cruel to widows or to children who have no family. Do not cheat foreigners or poor people. Do not think of ways to do bad things to each other.” ’
But the people did not obey the Lord 's commands. They turned away from him and they refused to listen to him.

They were too proud to accept his laws. They would not obey the messages that the Lord Almighty used his prophets to speak to them in past times. It was God's Spirit that spoke those messages through the prophets. Because of that, the Lord Almighty was very angry with them.
The Lord Almighty says, ‘When I called out to my people, they refused to listen to me. So when they called out to me, I refused to listen to them.

Instead, like a strong wind, I blew them away! I sent them to live among other nations that they did not know. The land that they left became an empty place where nobody lived. They caused a beautiful land to become like a desert.’
8: The Lord Almighty gave this message to me:
‘I love Zion very much because it is my special place. I am very angry with anyone who hurts my people who live there.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says.
The Lord says, ‘I will return to Zion and I will live in Jerusalem. Jerusalem will be called “City of Faithful People”. The mountain of the Lord Almighty will be called “God's Holy Mountain”.’
The Lord Almighty also says, ‘Old men and old women will walk in Jerusalem's streets with their walking sticks. They will sit and talk together in the public places.
Many boys and girls will play in the city's streets.’
The Lord Almighty says, ‘For the few people who remain in those days, this may seem an impossible thing to happen. But it is not a difficult thing for me to do.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says.
The Lord Almighty says, ‘I will rescue my people from the countries in the east and in the west.
I will bring them back to live in Jerusalem. They will be my people and I will be their God. I will rule them in a way that is faithful and right.’

The Lord Almighty also says, ‘You have heard the message that the prophets spoke when people were building the temple's foundation. So now be strong and brave! Finish building the house of the Lord Almighty.
Before that time, there was not enough money to pay for work that men or animals did. People could not travel safely anywhere because of their enemies. I caused people to hate each other.
But I will no longer do that to my people who remain. I will act differently from how I did in past times.

My people will be able to plant seeds safely in their fields. Their vines will provide plenty of grapes. Crops will grow well in their fields. There will be plenty of rain. My people who remain will enjoy all these things for themselves.

People of Judah and Israel, in past times other nations used your names as a curse. So now I will save you. You will provide a blessing to other nations instead of a curse. Do not be afraid! Be brave and strong.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says.
The Lord Almighty says, ‘When your ancestors made me angry, I decided to punish you. I would not change my mind about that.
But now I have decided to be kind to the people of Jerusalem and Judah. So do not be afraid!
These are the things that you must do. Each of you must speak true words to each other. In your courts, do what is right and fair.
Do not think of evil things that you can do to other people. Do not make false promises. All these things make me very angry.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says.
The Lord Almighty gave me another message.

He said, ‘There are special times each year when you fast. Those times are in the fourth, fifth, seventh and tenth months. Those times will now become happy feasts that the people of Judah should enjoy. So you must love truth and peace.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says.
The Lord Almighty also says, ‘One day, foreign people who live in many different cities will come to Jerusalem.

The people of one city will go and speak to the people of another city. They will say, “Come with us to Jerusalem to ask the Lord to bless us. We will go and worship the Lord Almighty. I am going there, so come with me.”
People from many places and from powerful nations will come to Jerusalem to worship the Lord Almighty. They will ask him to bless them.’

The Lord Almighty says, ‘At that time, people who speak many different languages will come from all other nations. Ten of them will go to one Jew and they will take hold of his coat. They will say to him, “People have told us that God is with you. So please let us go with you to worship him.” ’
9: This is a message from the Lord against the land of Hadrach. It speaks about the city of Damascus. Everyone looks to see what the Lord will do, including all the tribes of Israel.
The people of Hamath, which is near Damascus, also wait to see what the Lord will do. The people in the cities of Tyre and Sidon also do this, even though they are very wise.
Tyre's people have built strong walls around their city to keep it safe. They have stored a lot of silver and gold, as much as the dirt and mud in their streets!
But now the Lord God will take away all Tyre's valuable things. He will throw all its power into the sea! Fire will destroy the whole city.

The people in Ashkelon city will see what has happened to Tyre. They will be afraid. The people in Gaza city will shake with fear. The people in Ekron city will also be afraid. They will no longer hope for good things. The people of Gaza will no longer have a king. Ashkelon will become an empty place.
Foreign people will make Ashdod city their home. I, the Lord, will cause the proud Philistine people to be ashamed.

I will remove from their mouths the meat of sacrifices that they have offered to their idols. Those Philistines who remain will become a group of people who belong to me. They will be like a clan in the tribe of Judah. The Philistines of Ekron will join the people of Judah, as the Jebusites did long ago.
Then I will keep my temple safe. I will protect it like a guard. I will not allow an enemy to attack my people any more. Now I am watching carefully and I will take care of them.

Be happy and sing, people of Zion! Shout with joy, people of Jerusalem! Look! Your king is coming to you. He is righteous and he has won against his enemies. He is humble and he is riding on a donkey. Yes, he is riding on a young donkey.

I will remove the chariots from Ephraim. I will remove the soldiers' horses from Jerusalem, and I will destroy their bows and their arrows. The king will tell the nations that they will live in peace. He will rule as king from the sea in the west to the sea in the east. He will rule everywhere, from the Euphrates River to the furthest places on the earth.

I made a covenant with you, my people. The blood of sacrifices showed that you agreed with that covenant. Now some of you are prisoners. I will rescue those prisoners from dark places where there is no water.
Yes, you prisoners who now have hope of better things, return home where you will be safe. I tell you this today: I will give back to you twice as much as you have lost.

I will use Judah as my bow and Ephraim as my arrow to attack my enemies! I will use you, young men of Zion, to attack the soldiers of Greece. I will cause Zion to be like a soldier's sword in my hand.

Then the Lord will appear above his people. He will shoot his arrows like lightning in the sky. The Almighty Lord will make a loud noise with his trumpet. He will march into battle like a storm from the south.

The Lord Almighty will protect his people. They will attack their enemies with stones from their slings and they will win the battle. They will drink and they will shout loudly, like men who are drunk. The blood of their enemies will make them full, as a priest's bowl is full of blood when he pours it on the corners of the altar.
On that day, the Lord their God will rescue his people, as a shepherd rescues his sheep. They will live in his land, like bright jewels that shine in a crown.
It will all be very good and beautiful! Grain from the land will make the young men strong. The young women will become strong as they drink new wine.
10: Ask the Lord for rain in spring. It is the Lord who makes the clouds that give us rain. He will provide rain for everyone, so that crops grow in the fields.

When people ask their idols for help, the idols deceive them. People who use magic to know what will happen see false visions. People who explain the meaning of dreams tell lies. They cannot help anyone to be strong. As a result, people have nobody to show them the right way. They move around like sheep that have no shepherd to take care of them.

The Lord says, ‘I am very angry with the shepherds of my people. I will punish the leaders.’ Like a shepherd, the Lord Almighty takes care of his people. He will cause Judah's people to become strong, like horses that are ready to fight in a battle.

From among Judah's descendants a strong leader will appear. He is like the most important stone at the corner of a building. He is like a strong peg in a wall. He is like a bow that is ready to shoot arrows. Every strong ruler will come from Judah's descendants.
Together they will be like brave soldiers who stamp on their enemies in the mud of the streets. The Lord is with them as they fight. They will pull down the enemy soldiers who ride on horses.

The Lord says, ‘I will make Judah's people strong. I will rescue Israel's people. I will bring them back home, because I am sorry for them. It will seem as if I had never turned away from them. I am the Lord, their God, so I will answer their prayers.

Israel's people will be like brave soldiers. They will be as happy as men that have drunk wine. Their children will also see this and they will be happy too. They will sing with joy because of the things that the Lord has done.
I will call out to my people to come home. I have done what is necessary to rescue them. They will grow in number to be as many people as they were before.
I have sent them away to live among other nations. But they will remember me in those countries far away. They and their children will remain alive. They will return home together.
I will bring my people back from Egypt and from Assyria. I will bring them to live in Gilead and in Lebanon. Their own land will not be big enough for all of them.

When my people need to go across the sea of many troubles, I will cause its waves to stop. I will cause the deep water of the River Nile to become dry. Proud Assyria will become ashamed. Egypt will no longer have the power to rule other nations.
I will use my power to make my people strong. They will give me honour wherever they go.’ That is what the Lord says.
11: Open the gates of your land, Lebanon, so that the fire can destroy your cedar trees.
Cry aloud, pine trees, because the cedar trees have fallen. The great trees are completely spoiled. Cry aloud, Bashan's oak trees. Enemies have cut down the whole forest.
Listen to the shepherds as they cry aloud! Enemies have destroyed the fields where their sheep feed. Listen to the young lions as they roar! The trees of the Jordan Valley are completely spoiled.
The Lord my God said this: ‘You must take care of my people, as a shepherd takes care of his sheep. My people are like sheep that people will soon kill.

The people who buy the sheep will kill them. But nobody will punish them for this. The people who sell the sheep are happy. They say, “ Praise the Lord! Now I am rich.” Even the shepherds do not feel sorry for their own sheep.’

The Lord says, ‘I myself will no longer feel sorry for the people of this land. I will put them under the power of other people. I will put them under the power of their own king. Those powerful people will destroy the land, and I will not rescue the people.’

So I became a shepherd for the sheep that people would soon kill. I took good care of the weakest sheep. Then I took two shepherd's sticks. I called one stick ‘Kind’ and I called the other stick ‘Together’. I took good care of the sheep.
In one month, I removed the three other shepherds. I could not be patient with them and they hated me.

I said, ‘I will not be a shepherd for you any longer. If sheep are dying, I will allow them to die. If sheep are becoming lost, I will allow them to be lost. As for the sheep that still remain, they can destroy each other.’
I took hold of my stick that was called ‘Kind’. I broke it into two pieces. This showed that I had caused my covenant to finish. That was the covenant that I had agreed with all the people.
It was on that day that the covenant finished. The men who bought and sold sheep saw what I was doing. So they knew that it was a message from the Lord.
I said to them, ‘If you think it is right, you should pay me for my work. But do not pay me if you do not think it is right.’ So they decided to pay me 30 silver coins.
The Lord said to me, ‘Throw the money to the potter! They think that I am worth only 30 silver coins!’ So I took the money and I threw them to the potter at the Lord 's temple.
Then I broke the second stick into two pieces. That was the stick called ‘Together’. This showed that the kingdom of Judah would become separate from the kingdom of Israel.
Then the Lord said to me, ‘Take again the things that a foolish shepherd would use.

I will now bring another shepherd to rule in this land. He will not help the sheep that will soon die. He will not look for the sheep that are becoming lost. He will not help the sick sheep to become well again. He will not even feed the sheep that are healthy. Instead, he will eat the meat of the fat sheep. He will even pull off their feet for himself.

Terrible things will happen to the useless shepherd who leaves his sheep. His enemy's sword will cut his arm and his right eye. His arm will become weak and useless. His right eye will become completely blind.’
12: This is the Lord 's message that he spoke about Israel. He is the Lord who made all the heavens and who created the whole earth. He is the one who gives life to men and women. He says:

‘Look! I will cause Jerusalem to become like a bowl that is full of wine! The nations that are near will drink from it and they will become drunk. They will attack Jerusalem and the other cities of Judah.
On that day, I will cause Jerusalem to be like a heavy rock. Any army that tries to move it will hurt themselves very much. But all the nations of the earth will come together to attack Jerusalem.’
The Lord says, ‘On that day, I will cause every horse to become confused. I will cause their riders to be crazy. I will take care of Judah's people. But I will make the horses of their enemies blind.
Then the leaders of Judah will say to themselves, “Jerusalem's people are strong because the Lord Almighty is their God.”

On that day, I will make Judah's leaders like a flame that starts a fire in a heap of wood. They will be like a fire in a field of dry grass. They will destroy all the nations that are everywhere around them. Then the people of Jerusalem will live safely in the city.

I, the Lord, will save the homes of Judah first, before I rescue Jerusalem's people. As a result, the descendants of King David and the people of Jerusalem will not receive greater honour than other places in Judah.

On that day, the Lord will save the people who live in Jerusalem. Even the people who are very weak will become strong again, like great King David. King David's descendants will rule the people, as if the angel of the himself is leading them.
On that day, I will begin to destroy all the nations that attack Jerusalem.

I, the Lord, will pour a new spirit on David's descendants and on the people who live in Jerusalem. That spirit will cause them to be kind and to pray for help. So they will look towards me. They will see the one that they have pushed a spear into. They will be sad and weep, as parents weep after the death of their only son. They will weep loudly, as if their firstborn son has died.
On that day, the people in Jerusalem will weep very much. They will be as sad as the people were at Hadad-Rimmon, in the fields of Megiddo.

All the people in the land of Judah will weep in separate groups. One group will be the clan of David's descendants. Their wives will weep in another group. The clan of Nathan's descendants will weep together as a group. Their wives will weep in a separate group.
The clan of Levi's descendants will also weep as a group. And their wives will weep as a group. The clan of Shimei's descendants will weep as a group. And their wives will weep as a group.
All the other clans of Judah that still remain will also weep in groups, as well as their wives in separate groups.’
13: ‘On that day, water will start to pour from a fountain for King David's descendants and for Jerusalem's people. It will make them clean from their sins and the things that have made them unclean.’

The Lord Almighty says, ‘On that day, I will remove the names of idols from the land. Nobody will remember them any more. I will also remove from the land the false prophets and the bad spirit that deceives them.

Because of that, if anyone continues to prophesy, his own father and mother will say to him, “You must die. You say that you speak with the Lord 's authority, but you tell lies.” Then his own parents will use a sword to kill him as he prophesies lies.
On that day, every prophet will be ashamed of his visions. He will no longer wear a prophet's rough clothes to pretend that he is a prophet.
Instead, he will say, “Do not think that I am a prophet. No, I am a farmer. From the time that I was a boy, I have always worked on the land.”
People may ask him, “What caused those wounds on your body?” He will tell them, “I received those wounds when I was at the house of my friends.” ’
The Lord Almighty says, ‘Wake up, my sword! Attack my shepherd, the man who is near to me. Kill the shepherd and the sheep will run away. I will attack the little sheep.’
The Lord says, ‘In the whole land, only one third of the people will remain alive. Two-thirds of the people will die.

I will use fire to test those people who remain. I will make them pure, as men use fire to make gold and silver pure. They will call to me for help and I will answer them. I will say, “These are my people.” They will say, “The Lord is our God.” ’
14: Listen! A day will soon come when the Lord will judge people. At that time, Jerusalem's enemies will take your valuable things. They will share your things among themselves as you watch.

I will bring all the other nations together to attack Jerusalem. They will take the city for themselves. They will rob the houses. They will take hold of the women to have sex with them. They will take half the people away to other countries as their prisoners. They will allow the other people to remain in Jerusalem.
Then the Lord will prepare for battle. He will fight against the people of those nations, as he has fought battles in past times.

On that day, the Lord 's feet will stand on the Mount of Olives that is on the east side of Jerusalem. The Mount of Olives will break in two pieces. Half of the mountain will move north and half of the mountain will move south. There will be a great valley in the middle that goes from east to west.

The valley will reach as far as Azel. You will escape through the valley in the middle of the mountains. It will be like the time of the earthquake when your ancestors escaped. That happened when Uzziah was king of Judah. Then the Lord my God will appear. He will bring his holy angels with him.
On that day, there will be no light, as the sun will not shine in the day.
It will happen in one special day, a day that only the Lord knows. There will not be a normal day and a night. Instead there will be light at evening time.

On that day, fresh water will pour out from Jerusalem. Half of this water will go towards the sea in the east. Half of it will go towards the sea in the west. This will happen during both summer and winter.
The Lord will then be king to rule over all the earth. On that day, there will be only one Lord. Everyone will know him by that one name.

All the land will change. From Geba to Rimmon, south of Jerusalem, the land will become flat. But Jerusalem itself will rise up high above the land around it. It will remain in its own place, from the Benjamin Gate to the First Gate and on to the Corner Gate, and from the Tower of Hananel as far as the royal winepresses.
People will make their homes in the city. They will never be afraid of God's punishment again. Jerusalem will be a safe place to live.

But the Lord will punish the all nations that fought against Jerusalem. He will cause them to become very ill. Even as the people stand on their feet, their bodies will become weak and useless. Their eyes will become useless in their heads. Their tongues will become useless in their mouths.
On that day, the Lord will cause them to be afraid and confused. They will take hold of each other and fight against each other.

Judah's people will also go to Jerusalem to fight there. They will take for themselves the valuable things of all the nations around them. They will take gold and silver and lots of beautiful clothes.

The animals that belong to those other nations will also become ill, like their people. Their horses, their mules, their camels, their donkeys and all their other animals will be very ill in the places where they have their camps.

After that, the people who are still alive from the nations that attacked Jerusalem will return there. They will go to Jerusalem each year during the Festival of Huts. They will go to worship the King, the Lord Almighty.
If the people of any nation on the earth refuse to go to Jerusalem, their land will have no rain. They must go there to worship the King, the Lord Almighty.

If the Egyptian people refuse to go, their land will have no rain. Instead, the Lord will cause them to become very ill. That is how he will punish the people of any nation who refuse to go to Jerusalem for the Festival of Huts.
They must all go to worship the Lord during the Festival of Huts. The Egyptian people and the people of all the other nations will receive the same punishment if they do not do this.
On that day, the words, ‘Holy for the Lord ’ will be written on the bells of the horses. All the pots in the Lord 's temple will be as holy as the bowls in front of the altar.

All the pots in Jerusalem and in the whole of Judah will be holy things for the Lord Almighty. The people who come to offer sacrifices may use any of the pots to cook the meat for their sacrifices. On that day, there will no longer be any traders who buy and sell things in the temple of the Lord Almighty.
